title: Unsupported flag "--platform", multi arch support
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. ``docker run -it --name archlinux-cupsd --platform linux/amd64 archlinux``


**Describe the results you received:**

Unsupported flag "--platform"

**Describe the results you expected:**

Emulate target platform using Qemu. This seems to be an experimental feature on Docker but I would like to use it on Podman.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
ERRO[0000] cannot find mappings for user picloud: open /etc/subuid: no such file or directory 
Version:            1.9.2
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.14.2
Git Commit:         de2379a75863bdaf3ce8d414045d0b52c544f49a
Built:              Thu May 14 00:47:07 2020
OS/Arch:            linux/arm64
```

```

      host_id: 1002          
      size: 1                   
  kernel: 5.0.11-1-ARCH    
  memFree: 1498345472               
  memTotal: 4169707520
  ociRuntime:  
   name: runc                      
    package: Unknown                                                       
    path: /usr/bin/runc

      runc version 1.0.0-rc10
      commit: dc9208a3303feef5b3839f4323d9beb36df0a9dd

  os: linux
  rootless: true
  slirp4netns:
    executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns

      slirp4netns version 1.0.1
      commit: 6a7b16babc95b6a3056b33fb45b74a6f62262dd4
      libslirp: 4.3.0
  swapFree: 0
  swapTotal: 0
  uptime: 3h 20m 45.52s (Approximately 0.12 days)
registries:
  search:
  - docker.io
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
picloud@picloud ~> pacman -Q podman
podman 1.9.2-1

```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: Not going to be high on our priority list, but would welcome community to add patches.
Seems like a good project for an intrepid intern.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

@lsm5 are you interested in tackling the issue?

We can handle it entirely on the client side. Currently, `podman run` first checks if the image exists and pulls in case not. We shouldn't perform this check if --platform is specified and always pull in this case (image exists checks don't cover platform etc.). `--platform` is effectively a combination of `pull --override-arch ... --override-os`.
I think that's a cool feature for the qemu work etc.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
